questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In the circuit shown in the figure, the A.C. source gives a voltage $\mathrm{V}=20 \cos (2000 \mathrm{t})$ volt. Neglecting source resistance, the voltmeter and ammeter readings will be :-
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Here } \frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{LC}}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{5 \times 10^{-3} \times 50 \times 10^{-6}}}=2000 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s} \\ & \Rightarrow \omega=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{LC}}} \text { so resonance condition } \Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{\mathrm{V}}{\mathrm{R}}=\frac{20 / \sqrt{2}}{6+4}=\sqrt{2}=1.4 \mathrm{~A} \\ & \Rightarrow \text { Reading of ammeter }=1.4 \mathrm{~A} . \Rightarrow \text { Reading of voltmeter }=4 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~V}=5.6 \mathrm{~V}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In a RLC series circuit shown, the readings of voltmeters $V_1$ and $\mathrm{V}_2$ are 100 V and 120 V , respectively. The source voltage is 130 V . For this situtation mark out the correct statement (s)
options: Voltage across resistor, inductor and capacitor are 50 V , 86.6 V and 206.6 V , respectively
Voltage across resistor, inductor and capacitor are $10 \mathrm{~V}, 90 \mathrm{~V}$ and 30 V , respectively
Power factor of the circuit is $\frac{5}{13}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In a certain series LCR A.C. circuit it is found that $X_L=2 X_C$ and phase difference between the current and voltage is $\pi / 4$. If now capacitance is made $1 / 4^{\text {th }} \&$ the inductance and resistance are doubled then which are not correct.
options: voltage will lead current by a phase of $\tan ^{-1}(1 / 2)$.
voltage will lag behind current by a phase of $\tan ^{-1}(1 / 2)$.
voltage will lag behind current by a phase of $\pi / 4$.
voltage and current will be in same phase.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & X_L=2 X_C \\ & X_C^{\prime}=4 X_C \\ & X_L^{\prime}=2 X_L \\ & \Rightarrow X_L^{\prime}-X_C^{\prime}=2 X_L-4 X_C=2\left[X_L-2 X_C\right]=0\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
